description:
You are a storage administrator who has been asked to perform a very quick security-oriented disk-space check on the system.  
    Your manager is specifically worried about “world-writable” items (i.e. any file or directory whose UNIX permission bits are 777 or whose “others” field contains write-permission).  Attackers sometimes drop malicious payloads into such locations.

    1. Scan the entire /home/user hierarchy (do not look outside /home/user).  
    2. Find every regular file **or** directory that is world-writable.  
    3. When you are finished, create a plain-text report at  
       /home/user/security_scan/world_writable_report.txt  

       • Create the intermediate directory /home/user/security_scan/ if it does not already exist.  
       • The report must contain one insecure item per line.  
       • Each line must be in this exact format (no extra spaces or tabs):

         <absolute-path><single-space>:<single-space>world-writable

         Example of one correct line (do NOT hard-code this path):
         /home/user/example/path/file.txt : world-writable  

       • The lines must be sorted lexicographically (byte order, as produced by the standard `sort` command without extra options).  
       • Do not add blank lines at the top or bottom of the file.

    4. Make no other changes to the file system.

Automated grading will:
    • Verify that /home/user/security_scan/world_writable_report.txt exists,  
    • Verify that it is readable by the current user,  
    • Verify that its contents match the format above,  
    • Compare its sorted list of paths against the ground-truth list of world-writable items under /home/user.

A single, carefully-composed command is enough to accomplish the task, but you may use as many commands as you find convenient.  
Be sure your final answer includes the correctly-formatted report file in the precise location given above.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data for the autograder (NOT visible to the agent).
        Pre-existing insecure items present before the task starts:
            1. /home/user/shared_data/suspicious.log   (regular file, mode 0777)
            2. /home/user/shared_data/public           (directory,    mode 0777)

        No other files or directories under /home/user are world-writable.

        After the agent finishes, the file /home/user/security_scan/world_writable_report.txt
        must exist with the following exact contents (final newline allowed):

/home/user/shared_data/public : world-writable
/home/user/shared_data/suspicious.log : world-writable

        The two lines must be sorted in byte order, which puts “…public” before “…suspicious.log”.
    -->
name: task_000000_3a7c375b
